headline: Survey at 16% target with strong regional concentration; scaling urgency confirmed.
collection_status: 220 of 1,350 responses collected (16.3%). Lindi dominates (31% of sample) while Singida near-absent (0.5%). Geographic imbalance suggests uneven data quality and representativeness across the 10-region target.
key_findings: Expansion demand is clear: 77 respondents prioritise expanding KiuFunza, outweighing policy (48) and new programmes (48) combined—stakeholders want depth before breadth.
Scale readiness gap critical: average 2.9/5 indicates most stakeholders feel unprepared for expansion despite 4.4/5 satisfaction with partnership—capability lags appetite.
Teacher voice dominates (80 lower KF, 48 head teachers = 52% of sample); WEO, RC, DEO, regional officials represent <4% combined—district and regional oversight underrepresented.
Feedback themes centre on incentives, digital monitoring, government policy alignment, and WiGo expansion—consistent, actionable, but tied to systemic barriers outside KiuFunza control.
flags: Singida (1 response), Mtwara (12), Songwe (13) critically under-sampled; risk of invalid regional conclusions and missing minority perspectives.
Scale readiness 2.9/5 versus expansion priority 77 suggests optimism-capability mismatch; expansion without readiness builds on weak foundations.
Teacher over-representation (155/220 = 70%) skews findings toward classroom perspective; decision-maker and implementer voices (officials, volunteers) thin.
recommendation: Immediately prioritise collection in under-sampled regions (target 50+ per region) and deliberately oversample district/regional officials and volunteers to balance voice. Simultaneously commission rapid readiness diagnostics (systems, finance, training) in top 3 expansion-priority regions to bridge the 2.9 gap before scaling decisions.
